sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 17.119 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.17)

statement.latex: Suppose that a finite soluble group$G$admits a soluble group of automorphisms$A$of coprime order such that$C_G(A)$has rank$r$. Let$|A|$be the product of$l$not necessarily distinct primes. Is there a linear function$f$such that$G/F_{f(l)}(G)$has$(|A|,r)$-bounded rank, where$F_{f(l)}(G)$is the$f(l)$-th Fitting subgroup? An exponential function$f$with this property was found in (E.\,Khukhro, V.\,Mazu...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
